title: Protocol for Exo-CIP™ Rapid PCR Cleanup (#E1050)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"136gi","text":"Exo-CIP™ Rapid PCR Cleanup Kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"675pm","text":"Rapidly degrade residual PCR primers and dephosphorylate excess dNTPs after amplification","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"770na","text":"Reaction complete in 4 minutes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"73cj0","text":"Thermolabile formulation can be heat inactivated in 1 minute at 80°C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ai15l","text":"PCR product can be used directly in downstream applications","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9acva","text":"Compatible with commonly-used reaction buffers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3pfol","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transfer 5 µL, PCR product to a new PCR tube and add 1 µL, Exo-CIP A and 1 µL, Exo-CIP B . The final volume is 7 µL .
Mix thoroughly and briefly centrifuge at 1000 x g ..
Incubate the reaction tube for 00:04:00 at 37 °C followed by 00:01:00 at 80 °C .

Submit 3 µL, treated PCR product (in a range of 15-200 fmol) or less* for sequencing using BigDye™ Terminator v3.1 Cycle Sequencing Kit or store the treated samples at -20 °C for longer term storage. * A simple way to determine the amount of your amplicon is to load 3 µL on an agarose gel along with a known amount of a control DNA for comparison. Alternatively, direct measurement using fluorescent dye based kit (e.g., Qubit™) will ensure the proper amount of DNA is submitted. A B 1 Size of PCR amplicon ng of DNA (in 3 μl sample) 2 100 bp 1 - 12 3 500 bp 5 - 60 4 1000 bp 10 - 120 5 3000 bp 30 - 360 6 5000 bp 50 - 600
